title: bidslake ingestion schema
description: A bidslake-specific (not BIDS) schema that decides, for files already projected onto BIDS concepts, what bidslake does with each: read its contents into a data table, catalog it in the file registry, or ignore it. It selects with the BIDS selector-expression language over projected concepts (datatype/suffix/extension/…), and declares per-table policy for the data tables that readers populate. It replaces bidslake's previously-hardcoded read-vs-catalog logic (the `.tsv` gate, `.bval`/`.bvec` handling, recording/ordering rules).

rules.description: Ordered file-disposition rules; the first whose selectors all pass decides the file.

tables.description: Per-data-table policy (materialized concepts, row ordering), keyed by table name. Only tables that a reader populates need an entry; the file registry (`scans`) and BIDS entity tables are not configured here.

selectors.description: BIDS selector expressions over the file's projected concepts; all must pass for the rule to apply.

disposition.description: What bidslake does with a matched file.

oneOf.description: Parse the file's contents into rows of a data table via `reader` (e.g. a `.tsv` via `csv`, a FreeSurfer `.stats` via `fs_stats`). The file is also recorded in the registry, as with `catalog`.

Record the file as one row — its path plus projected BIDS concepts — in the standard file registry (`scans`, surfaced as the `files` view): the same registry populated for ordinary BIDS files, created even when no adapter is used. Contents are NOT parsed; the file stays on disk. This is how non-tabular data files (images, surfaces, `recon-all.log`) become discoverable.

Recognize the file but do nothing with it — neither read nor register it. The declarative way to exclude files (the role `.bidsignore` plays for BIDS tools): bidslake walks files a pipeline's `.bidsignore` hid, and `ignore` rules skip the ones that should stay out of the catalog.

reader.description: Reader name (required when disposition is `read`); validated against the compiled-in reader registry.

concepts.description: BIDS concept names materialized as physical columns on this data table, populated from the projection (e.g. `sub`, `ses`, `hemi`, `parc`). Presence marks the table as materialized-concept rather than the virtual-regex-over-path columns used by registry/BIDS-native tables — required because a non-BIDS path doesn't encode entities in its name.

ordered.description: Whether the source row order is load-bearing and must be preserved (rows read sequentially). When false, rows carry no meaningful order and may be read in parallel. Follows the BIDS row-order proposal (bids-standard/bids-2-devel#98); consumers sort by a canonical column (e.g. `onset`) when needed, so no sort column is specified.
